name: Bamboo Viscose Throw Blanket
editorialNote: Reese's most-reached-for reading companion. Temperature-regulating, impossibly soft, and the kind of thing you'll wrap yourself in every single night.
whyReeseLovesIt: Literally can't read without this. It's the softest thing I own.

name: Mokara Large Jar Candle
editorialNote: Clean, white floral — the scent that feels like a freshly pressed linen book page. Reese burns this every reading session.
whyReeseLovesIt: This is my reading candle. The scent is just pure calm.

name: Stagg EKG Electric Pour-Over Kettle
editorialNote: For the reader who takes their morning tea or coffee as seriously as their morning chapters. Precision temperature, beautiful design — the kind of thing you'll use every single day.
whyReeseLovesIt: My morning ritual starts here, then moves to whatever I'm reading.

name: Soft Blanket Large Jar Candle
editorialNote: Cotton-soft and cozy — the reading scent. Reese's go-to when she wants her living room to feel like a hug.
whyReeseLovesIt: This smells like everything cozy wrapped in one candle.

name: Monique Lhuillier Floral Mug
editorialNote: Reading is better with something warm in your hands and something beautiful in your sight line. This mug checks both boxes and then some.
whyReeseLovesIt: Life is too short for boring mugs. That's my whole philosophy.

name: The Body Serum
editorialNote: Reese's post-bath ritual before settling in with a good book. Niacinamide and peptides in the cleanest possible formulation.
whyReeseLovesIt: I put this on after a bath before I settle in to read. Perfect ritual.

name: Lip Sleeping Mask
editorialNote: The overnight essential that Reese swears by — slather it on before the last chapter and wake up with the softest lips of your life.
whyReeseLovesIt: I put this on every night before reading. Non-negotiable.

name: Alpha Beta Extra Strength Daily Peel
editorialNote: Two steps, two minutes — the peel Reese calls her skin's best friend. Used while winding down before a reading session.
whyReeseLovesIt: My skin changed. That's all I'll say. My skin changed.

name: Pure Radiance Oil
editorialNote: Clean, certified, and genuinely effective — the face oil Reese reaches for every evening. Because the right book and the right skincare are both acts of self-respect.
whyReeseLovesIt: Clean beauty that actually works. I've used this for years.

name: Essential for Women 35+ Multivitamin
editorialNote: Traceable ingredients, no unnecessary fillers — the multivitamin Reese takes as part of her morning ritual before the first coffee and the first chapter.
whyReeseLovesIt: I love knowing exactly what's in what I take. Ritual makes that easy.

name: Garden Party Softcover Journal
editorialNote: For capturing your thoughts between chapters, your favorite lines, and the moments when a book changes how you see the world. Beautiful enough to keep on your nightstand.
whyReeseLovesIt: I write in a journal every night. It's how I process what I read.

name: Pressed Wildflower Bookmark Set
editorialNote: Handcrafted resin bookmarks with pressed dried flowers. The kind of thing you'll use for every book and keep for years after.
whyReeseLovesIt: A beautiful book deserves a beautiful bookmark. Simple as that.

name: Daily Planner
editorialNote: For the woman who schedules her reading time the way she schedules everything else that matters. Cloth-bound, undated, built to last.
whyReeseLovesIt: I protect my reading time like a meeting. This is where I put it.

name: Blue Light Glasses
editorialNote: For the late-night reading sessions that go longer than intended. Protect your eyes in style — because reading until 2am is a lifestyle, not a problem.
whyReeseLovesIt: I started wearing these for late reading and my eyes are so much happier.

name: Intense Dark Chocolate Collection
editorialNote: A little treat to reward every 50 pages. Dark chocolate and literary fiction are, scientifically, one of the great pairings. Reese stocks these in every room she reads in.
whyReeseLovesIt: I reward myself with one square per chapter. It makes the reading even better.

name: Nomadic Chai Loose Leaf Tea
editorialNote: The chai that tastes like it was made specifically for an evening with a great book. Warming, complex, and impossible to rush — just like the best novels.
whyReeseLovesIt: My reading tea. I make a pot and settle in. Perfect every time.

name: Drizzle Extra Virgin Olive Oil
editorialNote: For the reader who takes their snack situation as seriously as their book selection. Squeeze bottle design, single-origin, outrageously good on everything.
whyReeseLovesIt: I put this on everything. My book club snack spread is not complete without it.

name: Cold Brew Coffee Concentrate
editorialNote: Morning reading fuel — smooth, strong, no bitterness. The cold brew Reese pours over ice before settling in for a long chapter.
whyReeseLovesIt: Morning reading needs good coffee. This is my go-to.

name: Bamboo Lounge Set
editorialNote: Reese's reading uniform: the loungewear so comfortable she's been photographed in it approximately one hundred times. Bamboo viscose, temperature-regulating, impossibly soft.
whyReeseLovesIt: This is what I wear every single weekend. Reading, family time, everything.

name: The Weekender Bag
editorialNote: The travel bag with a dedicated book pocket — because Reese never goes anywhere without at least two books. Structured, chic, and built for the reader who moves.
whyReeseLovesIt: There is literally a book pocket. That's all I need to say.

name: Elisa Initial Pendant Necklace
editorialNote: Reese's everyday jewelry. Delicate enough to layer, personal enough to feel like yours. The ideal gift for a reader who has everything except this.
whyReeseLovesIt: I wear a version of this almost every day. It's simple and perfect.

name: Ruffle Crew Socks
editorialNote: From Reese's own brand — the socks made for settling in with a great book. Cheerful, cozy, and the perfect small gift for any reader.
whyReeseLovesIt: Cozy socks are essential. These are my favorite pair I've made.

name: Fuzzy Cocoon Cardigan
editorialNote: The reading cardigan. Oversized, enveloping, the kind you throw on over pajamas and never want to take off. Reese considers this a reading essential, not a luxury.
whyReeseLovesIt: The reading cardigan. Everyone needs one. This is mine.

name: Linen Pillowcase Set
editorialNote: For the reader who inevitably falls asleep mid-chapter and wants the softest possible landing. Stone-washed linen, gets better with every wash.
whyReeseLovesIt: The best sleep is after a really good book. These make that sleep perfect.

name: Trigg Wall-Mounted Display
editorialNote: The sleek wall shelf that lets you display your current reads, back covers out — because books are beautiful objects and deserve to be shown off.
whyReeseLovesIt: I display my current reads on the wall. It's art AND function.
